symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive generalised proximal muscle weakness over ~1 year

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic, symmetrical, slowly progressive proximal weakness is the core clinical feature of sarcopenia.
Weakness is compatible but the year-long non-fluctuating course argues against a fatigable NMJ disorder.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute inability to rise from chair / bear weight after a fall

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Occult hip / neck-of-femur or pelvic fracture
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to weight-bear after a fall is a standard indication for hip/pelvis imaging irrespective of pain severity.
End-stage muscle loss can cross a threshold to complete loss of sit-to-stand power, but this is a diagnosis of exclusion here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced handgrip strength (weak grip, cannot open jars)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low grip strength is a defining EWGSOP2 criterion for sarcopenia.
Slow gait speed grades sarcopenia severity (physical performance domain).

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor protein/energy intake ('tea and toast') with weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inadequate protein intake is a direct driver of muscle catabolism and sarcopenia.
Poor intake predisposes to hypokalaemia, refeeding risk and dehydration/AKI.
Fatigue and exhaustion are components of the frailty phenotype.
Visible loss of muscle bulk supports low muscle quantity — but confirmatory quantification needs in-person exam/DXA, sealed from telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No bony point tenderness over hip/pelvis/spine; no pain on passive hip internal rotation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Occult hip / neck-of-femur or pelvic fracture
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of tenderness lowers fracture probability but does NOT exclude it; this examination cannot be performed via telehealth, so the AI cannot use it to clear the patient.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, urinary symptoms, chest pain or breathlessness

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Sepsis / acute infective cause of decline
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No infective features reduces (does not eliminate) an occult infective driver of functional decline in a frail elder.

symptoms.symptom_name: No new confusion; alert and oriented

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved cognition makes an acute septic/metabolic encephalopathy less likely but does not exclude occult sepsis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal reflexes, intact sensation, no upper-motor-neuron signs, symmetrical weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal neuro exam argues against cord compression — but this exam cannot be done via telehealth, so cord pathology stays unexcluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced appendicular skeletal muscle mass on DXA

investigation_details.expected_result: Low appendicular skeletal muscle mass index confirming sarcopenia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Sarcopenia cannot be formally confirmed/graded; used for diagnosis after acute causes excluded — not available in telehealth or acutely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low muscle quantity on DXA plus low strength confirms sarcopenia per EWGSOP2 — a sealed investigation result, never obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum electrolytes / renal function (FBC, U&E, calcium, magnesium, phosphate, vitamin D, CK)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Baseline bloods (FBC, U&E/creatinine, calcium, magnesium, phosphate, vitamin D, CK)
investigation_details.expected_result: To exclude hypokalaemia/hypocalcaemia/hypomagnesaemia, AKI, vitamin D deficiency and myopathy
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A reversible metabolic cause of weakness could be missed; cannot be obtained via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bloods are required to exclude a metabolic cause of profound weakness — a sealed clinician investigation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these establish the chronic sarcopenia phenotype — but they do not address the acute fall, which must be worked up independently.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Occult fracture / metabolic cause requiring in-person work-up
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Fall + inability to weight-bear + unexcluded metabolic derangement is the cluster that drives disposition; the physical exam and bloods that would clear it are unavailable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Hip and pelvis X-ray (CT if X-ray negative but suspicion persists)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Inability to weight-bear after a fall mandates imaging to exclude an occult neck-of-femur/pelvic fracture; requires in-person referral.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Baseline bloods (FBC, U&E/creatinine/eGFR, calcium, magnesium, phosphate, vitamin D, CK)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude a reversible metabolic cause of profound weakness and assess renal function / hydration in a malnourished elder.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: DXA for appendicular skeletal muscle mass
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and grades sarcopenia AFTER acute causes are excluded — not an acute or telehealth investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical repeat imaging or extensive remote work-up by the AI
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The AI cannot order or interpret imaging/bloods; the correct action is in-person referral, not remote investigation.
